## Excerpt (first 6 000 chars, docx)

__AFC LEARNING PATHWAYS__

__Structured AI Training, Certification and Progression Framework__

__Purpose\. __This document presents a practical learning pathway for AFC staff and leadership, showing how participants can progress from short, role\-based AI courses into examinable national qualifications, sector\-focused professional competence certificates and advanced AI degree progression\.

__AFC value proposition\. __The pathway is designed to support immediate productivity gains while creating a longer\-term skills pipeline for AI adoption, governance, automation, analytics and responsible innovation across AFC functions\.

# 1\. Proposed AFC Learning Pathway

__Level__

__Programme / Award__

__AFC Application__

__Credential__

Entry / Awareness

3\-Day UZ Courses

Mastering AI for Professionals, Executives and Directors

Certificate of Completion

Foundation

NFC in Artificial Intelligence

Baseline AI literacy, responsible use, productivity tools and introductory problem solving

HEXCO Qualification

Core Practitioner

NC in Artificial Intelligence

Applied AI, data use, workflow automation and organisational implementation

HEXCO Qualification

Advanced Practitioner

ND in Artificial Intelligence

Advanced AI applications, implementation projects and technical leadership

HEXCO Qualification

Specialisation

Professional Competence Certificates \(PCCs\)

Sector\-specific AI competence for areas such as procurement, finance, cybersecurity, HR, law, marketing, agriculture, mining and operations

HEXCO Professional Competence Certificate

Technical Skills

Technical Certification

Skills\-based technical validation for practical AI implementation roles

HEXCO Qualification / PCC

# 2\. Learning Pathway Diagram

The diagram below converts the AFC pathway into a progression model, showing the relationship between short courses, national qualifications, sector PCCs, technical certification and advanced AI degree progression\.
